btx-precision.opportunity: BTX Precision scores low on opportunity (20) with high risk (79) and elevated competitive pressure (71), driven by a quality escape, a lost recompete, and a competitor win. The lone positive is a $5.6M government contract award, but it is outweighed by contract loss and pricing pressure. This is a defend-and-stabilize situation: the account is under active threat and needs intervention before further erosion.

findings.sig-0001: A quality escape triggered a customer source-inspection hold—address the containment and corrective action immediately, as this drives both risk (+20) and competitive pressure (+10).
findings.sig-0002: Ironclad Components won a deal BTX was pursuing, the single largest competitive-pressure driver (+30)—expect further poaching attempts.
findings.sig-0003: Price increases citing material and labor inflation add risk (+9) and competitive pressure (+6), exposing BTX to being undercut on cost.
findings.sig-0004: A second round of inflation-driven price increases compounds pricing pressure (+15 risk, +10 competitive pressure)—reassess quote competitiveness.
findings.sig-0005: The $5.6M government contract award is the only opportunity driver (+20)—protect and leverage this win to offset losses elsewhere.
findings.sig-0006: Losing a $16.7M recompete held for years is a major risk driver (+20 risk, +15 competitive pressure)—understand why it was lost to prevent repeat.

titan-castings.opportunity: Titan Castings Co scored 0 on opportunity, driven entirely by risk (29) from three regulatory_change signals affecting their certified processes. With a 67% fit—BTX can serve their AS9100 and aluminum needs but has a NADCAP gap—this is a compliance-pressured supplier that may need a qualified partner as new requirements strain their certified processes. Approach them as a capacity or requalification backstop while they navigate regulatory changes.

findings.sig-0007: New compliance requirements are hitting Titan's certified processes—an opening for BTX to offer AS9100/aluminum support as they adapt.
findings.sig-0008: A second regulatory change adds pressure on their certified processes, reinforcing near-term need for a compliant machining partner.
findings.sig-0009: A third regulatory change signals sustained compliance disruption; note BTX's NADCAP gap may limit some scopes, so scope opportunities to AS9100/aluminum.

meridian-forgings.opportunity: Meridian Forgings scores 0 on opportunity, but that reflects their operational strain—not their fit. With an 80% fit to BTX Precision across 5-axis CNC, build-to-print, precision machining, and titanium, they are a strong technical match whose current capacity constraint (capacityRisk=35) and quality escape create an opening. The extended lead times and customer source-inspection hold signal that their customers are exposed and actively looking for relief, giving BTX a chance to capture displaced work.

findings.sig-0010: Meridian is quoting extended lead times because its floor is near capacity—BTX can offer available capacity to win overflow or displaced titanium, 5-axis, and build-to-print work.
findings.sig-0011: A quality escape triggered a customer source-inspection hold, meaning Meridian's customers are exposed and dissatisfied—BTX should approach those buyers with a reliable, precision-machining alternative.

cobalt-alloys.opportunity: Cobalt Alloys scored 0 on opportunity, driven entirely by risk signals: supplier delay, pricing pressure, and a quality escape push overall risk to 60, with capacity risk at 15 and competitive pressure at 20. Fit is 67% — they can support ITAR and aluminum work but have a NADCAP gap. This is a target because their operational instability creates openings to displace them with customers seeking a more reliable machining partner.

findings.sig-0013: Their multi-week shipment slip (supplier_delay+25, capacityRisk+15) means their customers are exposed to delivery gaps BTX can step in to fill.
findings.sig-0014: Their price increases citing material and labor inflation (pricing_pressure+15, competitivePressure+10) open the door for BTX to compete on more stable, competitive quoting.
findings.sig-0015: Their quality escape triggering a customer source-inspection hold (quality_escape+20, competitivePressure+10) signals frustrated customers BTX can win by emphasizing quality reliability.

granite-tooling.opportunity: Granite Tooling scores 0 as an opportunity, driven by high risk (53) and competitive pressure (20) with only 33% fit to BTX Precision. They can serve aluminum but have gaps in assembly and sheet metal, and the signals show repeated pricing pressure from material and labor inflation plus regulatory changes hitting their certified processes. This is a defensive-watch account rather than an active pursuit—monitor for disruption but do not prioritize outreach.

findings.sig-0016: Granite Tooling is raising prices due to cost inflation, signaling cost instability BTX should track but that also lowers their appeal as a partner.
findings.sig-0017: New compliance requirements are straining Granite Tooling's certified processes, adding regulatory risk to any engagement.
findings.sig-0018: A second regulatory-change signal reinforces that Granite Tooling's certifications face compliance disruption.
findings.sig-0019: Another price increase citing material and labor inflation confirms recurring pricing instability at Granite Tooling.
findings.sig-0020: A third pricing-pressure signal shows this cost inflation is a persistent pattern, not a one-off event.

apex-machining.opportunity: Apex Machining scores 87, driven by a stack of recent contract wins ($6.0M and $18.7M production deals) and three government awards ($10.6M, $6.1M, $1.7M), plus a facility expansion signaling capacity strain. Fit is strong at 80% — BTX covers 5-axis CNC, AS9100, aluminum, and titanium — with NADCAP as the only gap. The combination of surging demand and active capacity buildout makes Apex a prime overflow-capacity or subcontracting target now.

findings.sig-0021: Apex is expanding capacity, which means they're bumping against current limits — position BTX as overflow or subcontract capacity while their new build-out ramps.
findings.sig-0022: A $6.0M multi-year production win adds sustained volume BTX can support on 5-axis CNC, aluminum, and titanium work.
findings.sig-0023: A $10.6M government award brings compliance-heavy demand where BTX's AS9100 fit helps, though NADCAP gap should be flagged early.
findings.sig-0024: The $18.7M multi-year contract is the largest win and the biggest source of near-term capacity pressure — lead outreach with this.
findings.sig-0025: A $6.1M government award adds further backlog that could exceed Apex's in-house capacity, opening a subcontracting conversation.
findings.sig-0026: A $1.7M government award is smaller but reinforces a consistent inflow of work stretching Apex's throughput.

vanguard-aero.opportunity: Vanguard Aerospace scored 79, driven by a run of new contract wins ($12.2M, $6.0M, $3.3M) plus a $2.2M government award, all pointing to a demand surge that likely exceeds their current capacity. BTX fits at 75% and can serve their ITAR, build-to-print, and titanium needs, making Vanguard a strong subcontracting or overflow target despite being a competitor. A facility expansion adds competitive pressure but also signals they are scaling faster than they can absorb in-house.

findings.sig-0027: Their machining-capacity expansion confirms demand outpaces current capacity — position BTX as overflow/subcontract support before their new line is online.
findings.sig-0028: A $12.2M multi-year production win means sustained volume — pitch BTX for build-to-print or titanium work they can't fully absorb.
findings.sig-0029: An additional $6.0M multi-year contract deepens the backlog — reinforces overflow-capacity opportunity for BTX.
findings.sig-0030: A third $3.3M multi-year contract shows a stacking backlog — timing is right to offer BTX capacity now.
findings.sig-0032: The $2.2M government award confirms ITAR/defense work — BTX's ITAR capability makes it a viable qualified partner for that scope.

ironclad-comp.opportunity: Ironclad Components scored 58, driven by two multi-year production contract wins ($12.4M and $14.2M) plus a hiring surge signaling active growth. With 67% fit, BTX can serve their precision machining and titanium needs, though sheet metal remains a gap. The combination of new capacity commitments and expansion makes Ironclad a strong target for overflow or specialty supply partnerships.

findings.sig-0034: The $12.4M multi-year contract means Ironclad has committed capacity that may create overflow demand BTX can fill in precision machining or titanium.
findings.sig-0035: The second $14.2M multi-year contract compounds their production load, increasing the likelihood they need outside capacity where BTX's fit is strong.
findings.sig-0036: The hiring surge shows Ironclad is scaling to meet demand, a timing window for BTX to position as a supplemental supplier before they fully staff up.

strata-mfg.opportunity: Strata Manufacturing scores 68, driven by rapid demand growth: two multi-year production contracts ($6.0M and $7.5M) plus a $19.1M government award are stacking new work faster than existing capacity can absorb. With a 67% fit—BTX can serve their aluminum and build-to-print needs—Strata is a strong overflow and subcontracting target despite being a competitor. Their three announced facility expansions signal they're scrambling to add machining capacity, which is exactly the window to offer capacity relief before that new capacity comes online.

findings.sig-0037: A $6.0M multi-year contract means committed volume Strata must deliver—position BTX as build-to-print overflow to protect their delivery dates.
findings.sig-0038: A facility expansion for machining capacity confirms they're capacity-constrained now—pitch BTX for the gap before their new lines are running.
findings.sig-0039: A second expansion announcement reinforces the capacity crunch—there's an immediate need for outside aluminum machining support.
findings.sig-0040: A $7.5M multi-year contract adds more committed volume beyond current capacity—another lever for BTX to offer production support.
findings.sig-0041: A third expansion signal shows sustained demand outpacing capacity—the overflow opportunity is durable, not a one-off spike.
findings.sig-0042: A $19.1M government award brings strict schedule and compliance demands—BTX can be the reliable subcontractor that safeguards on-time delivery.

harbor-aero.opportunity: Harbor Aerosystems is an existing customer scoring a maxed-out 100 on opportunity, driven by two multi-year production contract wins ($4.1M and $19.3M), multiple demand spikes on new program starts, and back-to-back hiring surges — all signaling a real growth push. Fit is 67%: BTX can serve their AS9100 and aluminum needs, though sheet metal is a gap. With strong buying momentum and capacity risk still low at 16, this is a prime account to expand into now.

findings.sig-0043: A hiring surge signals Harbor is scaling up operations — engage early to position BTX capacity ahead of their ramp.
findings.sig-0044: A demand spike on new program starts means fresh part volumes are coming — pitch BTX's AS9100 aluminum machining for those programs.
findings.sig-0045: A second hiring surge reinforces sustained growth — treat this as an expanding, not one-off, opportunity.
findings.sig-0046: A second demand spike confirms multiple new programs — line up quotes on aluminum work while their need is peaking.
findings.sig-0047: A $4.1M multi-year contract win gives Harbor funded, recurring demand — target BTX as a supplier on that production run.
findings.sig-0048: A $19.3M multi-year contract win is the biggest driver of this account's value — prioritize winning machining scope tied to this program, noting sheet metal is a gap BTX can't cover.

continental-def.opportunity: Continental Defense is a maxed-out target: a perfect 100 opportunity score and 100% fit across all five of BTX's core capabilities (5-axis CNC, aluminum, build-to-print, precision machining, titanium) with zero gaps. The score is driven by a $14.0M multi-year contract win, a $19.3M government contract award, two demand spikes on new program starts, and a hiring surge — all pointing to near-term outsourced machining needs. A capacityRisk of 16 (both demand spikes) suggests they may be stretched and receptive to a partner who can absorb overflow.

findings.sig-0049: Their hiring surge signals a growth push — pitch BTX as the machining capacity they can bring online faster than internal headcount.
findings.sig-0050: A demand spike on new program starts means immediate part volume BTX can help fulfill before they hit capacity limits.
findings.sig-0051: The $19.3M government contract award drives defense-grade precision demand — position BTX's titanium and build-to-print capabilities against this program.
findings.sig-0052: A second demand spike confirms sustained program ramp and reinforces urgency for outsourced machining support.
findings.sig-0053: The $14.0M multi-year production contract signals long-term, recurring volume BTX can lock in as a preferred supplier.

northwind-sys.opportunity: Northwind Systems is a high-priority target with a maxed-out opportunity score of 100, driven by a $17.4M production contract win, three government contract awards totaling $24.7M, a demand spike on new program starts, and a hiring surge. This is a growing existing customer in Austin generating strong new-work demand. Note the critical caveat: fit to BTX Precision is 0% — we can serve none of their needs today due to gaps in NADCAP, assembly, and sheet metal, so any pursuit requires closing those capability gaps first.

findings.sig-0054: Their hiring surge signals a growth push, meaning rising future production volume that could translate into supplier demand if BTX can qualify.
findings.sig-0055: The demand spike on new program starts indicates near-term production needs — and drives a capacity-risk flag (+10), so watch for timeline and load pressure.
findings.sig-0056: The $17.4M multi-year production contract win means sustained, multi-year sourcing volume BTX could target — pending closing NADCAP/assembly/sheet metal gaps.
findings.sig-0057: The $2.7M government contract award adds regulated-work demand, which typically requires NADCAP certification BTX currently lacks.
findings.sig-0058: The $11.1M government contract award is a large regulated program creating significant supplier demand contingent on NADCAP qualification.
findings.sig-0059: The $10.9M government contract award further expands government-driven volume, reinforcing that NADCAP is the key gap to unlock this account.

beacon-aviation.opportunity: Beacon Aviation is a maxed-out opportunity at 100, driven by a $3.7M multi-year production contract win, a $5.8M government contract award, two demand spikes on new program starts, and a hiring surge — all pointing to rapid capacity expansion. Fit is partial at 50%: BTX can serve their titanium needs but has a gap in sheet metal. With capacityRisk at 16 from repeated demand spikes, Beacon likely needs outside precision-machining support now to keep up with committed work.

findings.sig-0060: A demand spike on new program starts signals near-term titanium machining volume BTX can supply.
findings.sig-0061: The $3.7M multi-year contract means sustained, funded production work — a durable revenue stream to attach to.

summit-turbine.opportunity: Summit Turbine scores 67 with a perfect 100% fit—BTX Precision can serve all their needs (5-axis CNC, AS9100, titanium) with no gaps. This is a strong, well-aligned target worth immediate outreach.

findings.sig-0066: A $4.4M government contract means Summit Turbine has funded work and budget for outsourced precision machining—lead with capacity to support this program.

delta-propulsion.opportunity: Delta Propulsion scores 35, driven by a demand spike (+25) from new program starts and a hiring surge (+10) signaling a growth push. With 80% fit, BTX Precision can serve their aluminum, build-to-print, precision machining, and titanium needs — though assembly is a gap. The demand spike also carries a capacity risk of 10, meaning Delta may be looking to offload work as internal capacity tightens.

findings.sig-0072: New program starts are spiking demand, so Delta likely needs outside precision machining capacity — a timely opening for BTX to win build-to-print work in aluminum and titanium.
findings.sig-0073: A surge in job openings shows Delta is scaling up and may struggle to staff fast enough, positioning BTX as a partner to absorb overflow machining demand.

orion-defense.opportunity: Orion Defense scores 61, driven by a demand spike on new program starts (+25) and a $4.1M government contract award (+20), with two hiring surges adding growth momentum (+10, +6). findings.sig-0074: New program starts mean Orion needs machining capacity now — lead with 5-axis CNC and aluminum build-to-print to capture early sourcing decisions.
